title: Machine learning-assisted pesticide detection on a flexible surface-enhanced Raman scattering substrate prepared by silver nanoparticles
authors: F. Sahin, N. Celik, A. Camdal, M. Sakir, A. Ceylan, M. Ruzi, M. S. Onses
abstract: We present a flexible SERS substrate prepared from silver nanoparticles combined with machine learning for highly sensitive and selective pesticide detection. The approach enables practical field-deployable sensing with improved accuracy over conventional methods.

title: Disintegration and Machine-Learning-Assisted Identification of Bacteria on Antimicrobial and Plasmonic Ag–CuxO Nanostructures
authors: F. Sahin, A. Camdal, G. Demirel Sahin, A. Ceylan, M. Ruzi, M. S. Onses
abstract: This work demonstrates antimicrobial Ag–CuxO nanostructures capable of both disintegrating bacteria and enabling machine-learning-assisted identification via SERS, offering a dual-function platform for rapid pathogen detection.

title: Indomethacin prevents TGF-β-induced epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition in pancreatic cancer cells; evidence by Raman spectroscopy
authors: G. Sezer, M. S. Onses, M. Sakir, F. Sahin, A. Camdal, Z. Sezer, A. Inal, Z. Ciftci
abstract: Using Raman spectroscopy, we provide molecular-level evidence that indomethacin inhibits TGF-β-induced epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition in pancreatic cancer cells, suggesting its potential as a therapeutic agent.

title: Practical SERS substrates by spray coating of silver solutions for deep learning-assisted sensitive antigen identification
authors: F. Sahin, G. D. Sahin, A. Camdal, I. Akmayan, T. Ozbek, S. Acar, M. S. Onses
abstract: We report a scalable spray-coating method to fabricate SERS substrates from silver solutions, combined with deep learning for accurate and sensitive antigen identification, enabling low-cost diagnostic applications.

title: An Assessment of the Application of Real-time YOLOv5 Deep Learning Algorithm in Unmanned Surface Vessels for Environmental Maintenance: Some Preliminary Results
authors: A. Camdal, B. Basu, A. Stanio
venue: Proceedings of the 32nd European Safety and Reliability Conference (ESREL 2022)
abstract: We assess the real-time performance of YOLOv5 for detecting and tracking debris on water surfaces from an Unmanned Surface Vessel, with AI and embedded applications built from scratch in C++, Python, and PyTorch.
